untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of multi-bar peak/trough divergence patterns (3+ successively higher/lower peaks/troughs with 5+ bar separation and 2% magnitude thresholds). This requires pattern matching logic across historical bar sequences and comparative peak/trough detection that cannot be expressed using only single-bar comparisons with allowed operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is). The backtest engine would need custom divergence detection functions to identify peak/trough sequences and measure relative magnitudes across multiple bars.
translation_notes: Signal is fundamentally about divergence pattern recognition requiring multi-bar state tracking and comparative analysis of indicator vs price peak/trough sequences. The provided operator set and single-condition structure cannot capture the sequential pattern matching needed. A simplified approximation using reversal logic has been provided but does not capture the divergence essence of the original signal.
